items.title: feat: improve typing of ImmutableDictList
items.body:
This PR aims at making the ImmutableDictList and NamedGridList more typesafe.

This is done mainly by making ImmutableDictList a generic that inherits from Mapping:

```python
class ImmutableDictList(Mapping[Hashable, V], Generic[V]):
  ...
```

Inheriting from Mapping changed the behavior for iterating the ImmutableDictList. For mappings it is default that the standard iterator yields keys, the ImmutableDictList yielded the values. So every time a standard iterator was used (for example in enumerate), the `.values()` function needs to be used now.

Also `.iteritems` was removed in favor of the standard `.items` method.

`__str__` was replaced by `__repr__`.

items.title: Wigner function implementation from Fock state tensor
items.body: 1. Fix bugs in ``BosonicState.wigner`` function - Error when computing the Wigner function of a Gaussian state with ``ncom=1`` -Solution: Squeeze ``exp_imag`` along the last two dimensions to ensure correct broadcasting behavior. - Unnormalized Wigner function values - Solution: Apply an explicit renormalization factor A comparison with the reference implementation in QuTiP is as follows, 2. Add function ``fock_wigner`` for Wigner function implementation from Fock state tensor - Example ``` device = 'cpu' r=1 d=0 cutoff = 30 cir1 = dq.QumodeCircuit(nmode=1, init_state='vac', backend='fock', basis=False, cutoff=cutoff) cir1.s(0, r=r, theta=0) cir1.d(0, r=d) cir1.ps(0, np.pi/3, encode=True) cir1.to(torch.double) cir1.to(device) re = cir1(data=torch.tensor([[0.],[0.],[0.]], dtype=torch.double, device=device)) s = dq.FockState(state=re, basis=False) w = s.wigner(wire=0, xrange=10, prange=10, npoints=100, k=0) ```

items.title: fix: some issues with soft_delete
items.body:
1. Closing OpenSearch Indices on Soft Delete:
   * Updated `SketchResource.delete` in `timesketch/api/v1/resources/sketch.py` to close all associated OpenSearch indices when a sketch is soft-deleted. This saves cluster resources.

2. Allowing Force Deletion of Soft-Deleted Sketches:
     * Modified `get_with_acl` in `timesketch/models/__init__.py` to accept an include_deleted parameter.
         This allows retrieving soft-deleted objects if the user is an admin and explicitly requests it.
      * Updated SketchResource.delete in `timesketch/api/v1/resources/sketch.py` to move the force_delete
         detection logic earlier and pass it to `get_with_acl(include_deleted=force_delete)`. This ensures
         that admins can find and permanently delete sketches that are already soft-deleted.


And adding e2e tests and documentation updates.

This PR is dependend on https://github.com/google/timesketch/pull/3661

fixes #3689

items.title: Fix xrange NameError by pinning PyPDF2>=3.0.0
items.body:
`NameError: name 'xrange' is not defined` occurs when older PyPDF2 versions are installed. These versions contain Python 2 code (`xrange` was renamed to `range` in Python 3) and lack the `PdfReader` class used by the app.

### Changes
- Pin `PyPDF2>=3.0.0` in `requirements.txt` to ensure Python 3 compatible version
- Add `__pycache__/` to `.gitignore`

items.title: Fix Flatpak build for offline SDK environment
items.body:
Flatpak build was failing due to missing dependencies and packages incompatible with the offline build environment and modern SDK toolchain.

## Changes

### hidapi C library
- Add `-DHIDAPI_WITH_LIBUSB=OFF` — SDK lacks `libusb-1.0`, hidraw backend sufficient for HID

### cython-hidapi
- Patch `setup.py` to remove pinned `Cython==0.29.25` — SDK provides 0.29.37
- Add `--without-libusb` to skip libusb backend requiring `libusb.h`

### simpleeval
- Switch from old git archive to PyPI wheel (1.0.3) — `use_2to3` removed in setuptools 58+

### fbs/future
- Add as separate modules with pre-downloaded sources — no network in Flatpak builds
- Install fbs with `--no-deps` — only `fbs_runtime` needed, not PyInstaller build tools

```yaml
# Key manifest changes
- name: hidapi
  config-opts:
    - -DHIDAPI_WITH_LIBUSB=OFF

- name: python3-hidapi
  build-commands:
    - sed -i "s/setup_requires = \['Cython==0.29.25'\]/setup_requires = []/" setup.py
    - python3 setup.py install --without-libusb --prefix=${FLATPAK_DEST}

- name: python3-fbs
  build-commands:
    - pip3 install ... --no-deps  # skip PyInstaller dependency
```

items.title: Add live web dashboard for system monitoring
items.body:
## Summary
- Standalone Node.js/TypeScript web dashboard (`dashboard/`) with Express + WebSocket backend and React + Tailwind frontend
- **Kanban view** with task-centric output streaming — click any In Progress, Completed, or Failed card to see its Claude output in a split panel
- **Overview tab** with queue depths, active locks, consumer groups, and stuck task alerts
- Stuck task detection: orphaned pending markers, high attempt counts, stale consumer group entries
- Multi-project support via wildcard Redis key patterns (`*:tasks:*`, `*:lock:*`, etc.)
- Separate Docker container on `orcest` network (read-only Redis access, 512MB limit)
- Makefile targets: `build-dashboard`, `dev-dashboard`, `deploy-dashboard`

## Architecture
```
Browser  ←WebSocket→  Express + ws (Node container)  ←ioredis→  Redis
                      serves static Vite build
```

## Test plan
- [ ] `cd dashboard && npm ci && npm run build` — server + frontend build clean
- [ ] `cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit` — no type errors
- [ ] `docker build -t orcest-dashboard ./dashboard` — image builds
- [ ] Run alongside orchestrator via `docker-compose.dashboard.yml` — accessible on :8080
- [ ] WebSocket connection indicator shows "Connected"
- [ ] Overview tab shows live queue depths, locks, consumer groups
- [ ] Kanban tab shows cards in Queued/In Progress/Completed/Failed columns
- [ ] Clicking an In Progress card opens live task output panel
- [ ] Clicking a Completed/Failed card shows historical task output

items.title: Remove Python 2 compatibility code
items.body:
Project requires Python ≥3.8 but retained Python 2 compatibility layer. Removed obsolete compatibility code.

## Changes

**Removed `six` dependency:**
- Replaced `six.string_types` → `str`
- Replaced `six.text_type` → `str`
- Eliminated `six.PY2` conditionals (kept Python 3 paths)
- Replaced `six.moves` imports with stdlib equivalents

**Simplified carbonlink unpickler:**
- Removed dual-implementation SafeUnpickler class
- Direct module name mapping (`__builtin__` → `builtins`, `copy_reg` → `copyreg`)

**Cleaned vendored whisper.py:**
- Removed `izip`/`ifilter`/`xrange` compatibility shims
- Direct use of native `zip`/`filter`/`range`

**Removed future imports:**
- Deleted `from __future__ import absolute_import`

## Example

Before:
```python
import six
from six.moves import cPickle as pickle

if isinstance(windowSize, six.string_types):
    delta = parseTimeOffset(windowSize)
```

After:
```python
import pickle

if isinstance(windowSize, str):
    delta = parseTimeOffset(windowSize)
```

Net change: 10 files, -67/+38 lines

items.title: Update & improve docs
items.body:
This pull request streamlines and modernizes the documentation for hvantk by simplifying contribution instructions, condensing and updating the README's workflow examples, improving architectural documentation, and clarifying module structure. The main changes focus on making onboarding easier, documentation more maintainable, and the architecture clearer for new contributors and users.

**Documentation simplification and restructuring:**

* Replaced the detailed `CONTRIBUTING.md` with a pointer to the new, full guide at `docs_site/contributing.md` for easier maintenance and centralized updates.
* Updated the quick start and workflow sections in `README.md` to concise command-line examples for key pipelines, referencing the Quick Start Guide for details. Removed verbose step-by-step examples and moved detailed documentation links to the docs site.
* Corrected the "Data Sources" documentation link in the README to point to `guide/data-sources.md` instead of `guide/annotation-sources.md`.

**Architecture documentation improvements:**

* Replaced the embedded Mermaid diagram in `docs_site/architecture.md` with a static SVG image and a concise caption, making the architecture easier to view and maintain.
* Updated the project structure section to reflect new and reorganized modules, including the addition of streamer and dataset files, and clarified the location of gene mapping utilities. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7c3ecaa08aa37be02130a9a783174a6aab62ed722d3ce833436ce2afbf47fe5dL132-R42) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7c3ecaa08aa37be02130a9a783174a6aab62ed722d3ce833436ce2afbf47fe5dL178) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-7c3ecaa08aa37be02130a9a783174a6aab62ed722d3ce833436ce2afbf47fe5dL209-R113)
* Modernized the description of core protocols (Builder, Streamer, Downloader) to use current code patterns and clarify best practices, including functional builder patterns and dataclass-based downloaders.
* Clarified CLI and test organization, reflecting the modular structure and new test layout. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7c3ecaa08aa37be02130a9a783174a6aab62ed722d3ce833436ce2afbf47fe5dL451-R369) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7c3ecaa08aa37be02130a9a783174a6aab62ed722d3ce833436ce2afbf47fe5dL479-R404)
* Removed the "Future Directions" section from the architecture doc, focusing the document on current features and references.

**Minor improvements:**

* Updated the development environment setup instructions in the contributing guide to use `eval $(poetry env activate)` for better shell integration.

Expands from 3 Capital Region districts to 74 districts statewide, grouped by BOCES region. Adds cluster comparison views with regional benchmark overlays and 92 tests.

### Districts & Data
- `config/districts.json`: 74 districts with `boces` field across 18 BOCES regions (Capital Region, Nassau, Suffolk, Westchester, Monroe, Erie, Onondaga, etc.)
- Seed data expanded for all districts (assessments, levy, sources)

### BOCES Clustering (`build_specs.py`)
- `compute_boces_benchmarks()` — averages proficiency/levy across each BOCES region
- `build_boces_cluster_spec()` — multi-district comparison charts with dashed benchmark lines
- Spec index now includes `boces` array alongside `districts`

### Dashboard UI
- BOCES filter dropdown narrows district selector
- "Compare BOCES Districts" button loads cluster view with regional benchmark overlay
- Line chart renderer handles string x-axis (fiscal years) and dashed series for benchmarks

// Category x-axis detection for cluster levy charts

}

TypeError: One of the data arrays does not have 32-bit float type
```

---
## Cause

I check the types of `pos, vel, elem_den, temp, hh` and elem_den returns float64 instead of 32, causing mismatch.
Simliar for ratenetworkspectra.py there are mismatch in density and lienergy.

I suspect somewhere in the code the float32 rule is more strict than 2023 version (or maybe numpy is more restrict), but I am not familiar with the code enough to pinpoint that. So perhaps there are multiple occurrences need to patch

## Fix

Current fix for me is change the dtype of `self.rscale` from float64 to float32, so it won't affect the downstream `elem_den`.
And for `ratenetworkspectra.py` the fix is making these two float32
```python
       ldensity = np.log(density).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
        lienergy = np.log(ienergy).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
```
copy false intentionally avoid creating new copy.

items.title: Migrate checkpoints to per-commit granularity with API proxy transcript capture
items.body:
## Summary

Migrates checkpoint system from per-push to per-commit granularity and replaces Claude Code JSONL transcript extraction with Anthropic API proxy instrumentation.

Key changes:
- **Per-commit checkpoints**: Each commit in a push gets its own checkpoint, enabling precise traceability between code changes and agent sessions
- **API proxy transcript capture**: Transcripts are now extracted from API request/response pairs captured at the gateway proxy layer, providing a stable format independent of Claude Code internals
- **Buffer rotation**: 10MB per-session buffer with rotation to handle long sessions
- **Graceful degradation**: Checkpoint creation continues even if transcript extraction fails

## Architecture

```
gateway.py (push) → checkpoint_handler → transcript_extractor → proxy buffer
                          ↓
               checkpoint branch (egg/checkpoints/v1)
```

The proxy buffer (`/tmp/egg-transcripts/{container_id}.jsonl`) captures:
- API request/response pairs (messages, tools, content)
- Token usage per request  
- Tool calls and their results
- Both streaming and non-streaming responses

## Files Changed

**New files:**
- `gateway/transcript_buffer.py` - TranscriptBuffer class for API traffic capture
- `gateway/tests/test_transcript_buffer.py` - 19 tests
- `gateway/tests/test_checkpoint_handler.py` - 15 tests
- `tests/shared/egg_contracts/test_transcript_extractor.py` - 17 tests

**Modified:**
- `gateway/gateway.py` - Instrumentation hooks for API proxy
- `gateway/checkpoint_handler.py` - Per-commit iteration, proxy buffer integration
- `gateway/session_manager.py` - Buffer cleanup on session end
- `shared/egg_contracts/transcript_extractor.py` - Rewritten for proxy buffer only
- `shared/egg_contracts/checkpoint_cli.py` - Updated help text

Issue: closes #509

## Test plan

- [x] All 944 tests pass
- [x] Per-commit iteration tested with mocked git rev-list
- [x] Buffer rotation tested at size limits
- [x] Transcript extraction tested for messages, tool calls, token usage
- [x] SSE streaming response parsing tested
- [x] Concurrent buffer writes tested for thread safety

items.title: Fix Python 3 compatibility and Docker environment detection
items.body:
AutoSploit has Python 2 syntax incompatible with Python 3 and loops infinitely when checking for local services in Docker containers where PostgreSQL runs externally.

## Python 3 Compatibility

- `autosploit/main.py`: `print error_traceback` → `print(error_traceback)`
- `lib/output.py`, `lib/jsonize.py`: `raw_input()` → `input()`
- `lib/term/terminal.py`: Fixed backwards compat shim (`raw_input = input`, not `input = raw_input`)
- `lib/creation/issue_creator.py`: Binary mode for hashlib (`open(path, 'rb').read()`)

## Docker Environment Detection

Skip local service checks when running in Docker:

```python
is_docker = os.path.exists("/.dockerenv") or os.environ.get("POSTGRES_HOST")

if is_docker:
    info("running in Docker environment, skipping local service checks")
else:
    # original service check logic
```

## Docker Build Simplification

- `Dockerfile`: Remove `fix_autosploit.py` dependency, fixes now in source
- `entrypoint.sh`: Simplified startup, no local PostgreSQL (uses external `msfdb` container)
- Deleted `fix_autosploit.py`

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

>

items.title: Update massweb to Python 3 and fix vulnerability detection bugs
items.body:
## Overview

This PR migrates the massweb vulnerability scanner from Python 2.7 to Python 3 and fixes critical bugs that prevented proper vulnerability detection and reporting.

## Issues Fixed

### Critical Bug: XSS Detection Not Working
The XSS vulnerability checker was failing to detect cross-site scripting attacks because the `html5lib` parser strips script content by design for security reasons. This meant that payloads like `<script>alert(31337)</script>` were never being detected.

**Fix**: Switched to `html.parser` for script tag detection in `parse_match()`, which preserves script content and allows proper XSS detection.

### Syntax Error in XPath Checker
Missing comma on line 24 of `xpathi.py` caused a syntax error where two string literals were being concatenated instead of being separate list items.

## Python 2 to 3 Migration

Comprehensive updates to support Python 3.7+ including:

### Import Updates
- `urlparse` → `urllib.parse`
- `urllib` → `urllib.parse`
- Removed deprecated `sets` module

### Language Changes
- Converted all `print` statements to `print()` functions
- Replaced `.iteritems()` with `.items()`
- Replaced `basestring` with `str`
- Replaced `unicode()` with `str()`
- Updated `sys.stdin`/`sys.stderr` encoding for Python 3 text streams

### API Updates
- Updated BeautifulSoup: `findAll()` → `find_all()`

## Files Changed

Updated 17 files across the codebase:
- Core vulnerability checkers (`xpathi.py`, `match.py`)
- Fuzzing engines (`web_fuzzer.py`, `bsqli_fuzzer.py`)
- HTTP request handling (`mass_request.py`, `pnk_request.py`)
- URL generation and parsing
- Hadoop utilities
- Test files

## Testing

All vulnerability detection tests now pass:
- ✅ XPath Injection detection
- ✅ SQL Injection detection
- ✅ Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) detection
- ✅ Path Traversal detection
- ✅ Mail Form Injection detection
- ✅ OS Command Injection detection

Package successfully installs and runs on Python 3.7 through 3.12.

## Python Version Support

Updated `setup.py` classifiers:
- **Removed**: Python 2.7
- **Added**: Python 3.7, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12

The scanner is now fully operational with modern Python versions and correctly reports vulnerabilities as intended.

items.title: Collections no case multi dict
items.body:
NoCaseMultiDict:

Dictionary with case-insensitive keys and support for multiple values per key. The first inserted key spelling is preserved.

inherit from `MutableMapping[str, V], Generic[V]` to make it more typesafe - but why not `MutableMapping[str, list[V]]?

Because `__getitem__` (`[]` access) returns just the first value!

The special method `get_all` is used to get all values for one key.

Inheriting from `MutableMapping[str, V], Generic[V]` breaks the signature of the custom `update` method so a new `update_multi` method is introduced.

It als breaks the signature of `iteritems` (becoming `items`), in the old version it yielded `(str, list[V])`, but with `Mapping[str, V]` it is only allowed to yield `(str, V)`. So a new `items_multi` method is introduced.

Overall this changes make the standard API behave more consistent (signatures of `__getitem__`, `update` and `items` fit together).

Also the `type_func` for the `get` method is also breaking the api. As it is very easy to replace `ncmd.get('x', type_func=int)` with `int(ncmd.get('x'))`, I just did that.

items.title: Drop Python 2.7 support and modernize codebase
items.body:
### TL;DR

Drop Python 2.7 support and modernize the codebase to require Python 3.8+

### What changed?

- Removed Python 2.7 from CI test matrices in both GitHub Actions workflows
- Upgraded GitHub Actions from v2 to v4 for artifact upload/download
- Replaced `pkg_resources` with `importlib.metadata` for version detection
- Removed Python 2/3 compatibility code including `izip`, `basestring`, and `PY2`/`PY3` checks
- Simplified async support by making `RtlSdrAio` the default `RtlSdr` class
- Removed conditional librtlsdr building and Python 2-specific dependencies
- Updated package classifiers and added `python_requires = >=3.8` in setup.cfg
- Removed Python version checks from test configuration

### How to test?

Run the existing test suite on Python 3.8+ to ensure all functionality works correctly. The CI workflows will now only test against Python 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, and 3.11 on all supported platforms.

### Why make this change?

Python 2.7 reached end-of-life in January 2020 and is no longer supported. This modernization removes legacy compatibility code, simplifies the codebase, and allows the project to take advantage of modern Python features while reducing maintenance burden.

items.title: Refactor SQL Agent Architecture for Self-Correction and Robustness
items.body:
This PR implements the architectural feedback to improve the SQL agent's robustness.

Changes:
1.  **State Management**: `AgentState` now tracks `raw_input` (immutable) and `enhanced_input`. It also tracks `sql_query`, `sql_result`, `error`, and `retry_count`.
2.  **Cyclic Graph**: The linear chain is replaced with a cyclic graph in `workflow.py`. A conditional edge `conditional_edge_check_result` checks for errors and routes back to `node_sql_generator` for retries (up to 3 times).
3.  **Separation of Concerns**: SQL generation and execution are now in separate nodes (`node_sql_generator` and `node_sql_executor`).
4.  **Self-Correction**: `node_sql_generator` now accepts `previous_error` and `previous_sql` if they exist in the state, allowing the DSPy model to fix its own errors.
5.  **Robustness**: `node_sql_executor` strips markdown blocks from the generated SQL. `node_table_router` uses token-based matching for owner hints.
6.  **Initialization**: `main.py` is updated to pass the full initial state structure to the graph.

items.title: MYTUBE-18 Build Cloud Run Job for FFmpeg HLS transcoding and thumbnail extraction
items.body:
## Issues/Notes

- `CDN_BASE_URL` must be set before the Cloud Run Job can write thumbnail URLs to the DB. The placeholder value in `cloudjobs.yaml` is empty — a reviewer must populate it with the actual CDN hostname before deploying.
- `gcs_client.go` (thin delegating wrappers over the real GCS SDK) has 0% unit test coverage by design — these require a live GCS connection and are not suitable for unit testing. All logic using them is covered via interface stubs.
- The existing `unit-tests.yml` `test-api` job already runs `go test ./...` from the `api/` directory, which covers `cmd/transcoder/...`. No new CI job was needed.

## Approach

The transcoder is a Go binary at `api/cmd/transcoder/main.go` (Q: MYTUBE-57, Option A) sharing the `api` Go module. This allows reuse of `api/internal/database` for DB connection.

**Q&A decisions applied:**
- **DB connection** (MYTUBE-55): Cloud SQL Unix socket via `INSTANCE_UNIX_SOCKET` + shared `DB_USER`/`DB_PASSWORD` secrets — same pattern as the API service.
- **Thumbnail URL** (MYTUBE-56): `CDN_BASE_URL` env var injected into the job; URL written as `${CDN_BASE_URL}/videos/{VIDEO_ID}/thumbnail.jpg`.
- **Language** (MYTUBE-57): Go binary in `api/cmd/transcoder/` — shares module, enables unit testing, reuses DB helpers.
- **Ephemeral disk** (MYTUBE-58): 10 GiB — set in `cloudjobs.yaml` and `setup.sh`.

**Architecture:** Single Responsibility + Dependency Injection via interfaces. The core `transcode()` function accepts injected interfaces for all I/O, enabling full unit testing without real GCS or FFmpeg.

Pipeline steps in `doTranscode()`:
1. Download raw file from GCS to ephemeral disk
2. Run FFmpeg → multi-bitrate HLS (360p/500k, 720p/1500k, 1080p/3000k) with master playlist `index.m3u8`
3. Extract thumbnail via FFmpeg `-ss 5 -frames:v 1`
4. Upload HLS dir to `gs://HLS_BUCKET/videos/{VIDEO_ID}/`
5. Upload thumbnail to `gs://HLS_BUCKET/videos/{VIDEO_ID}/thumbnail.jpg`
6. Update DB: `hls_manifest_path`, `thumbnail_url`, `status = ready`

On any failure, `transcode()` calls `repo.MarkFailed()` before returning the error.

## Files Modified

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `api/cmd/transcoder/main.go` | Created | Job entry point; wires real GCS/DB/FFmpeg deps; `transcode()` + `configFromEnv()` |
| `api/cmd/transcoder/internal/ffmpeg/runner.go` | Created | FFmpeg HLS transcoding + thumbnail extraction with `CommandRunner` interface |
| `api/cmd/transcoder/internal/storage/gcs.go` | Created | `Downloader` + `Uploader` using `ObjectReader`/`ObjectWriter` interfaces |
| `api/cmd/transcoder/internal/storage/gcs_client.go` | Created | Real GCS SDK adapters implementing `ObjectReader`/`ObjectWriter` |
| `api/cmd/transcoder/internal/video/repository.go` | Created | `Repository` with `UpdateVideo()` and `MarkFailed()` using `database/sql` |
| `api/cmd/transcoder/Dockerfile` | Created | Multi-stage build; alpine + `apk add ffmpeg` |
| `api/go.mod` / `api/go.sum` | Modified | Added `cloud.google.com/go/storage` dependency |
| `infra/cloudjobs.yaml` | Modified | Added `RAW_BUCKET`, `CDN_BASE_URL`, DB env vars, 10 GiB ephemeral disk |
| `infra/setup.sh` | Modified | Updated `gcloud run jobs create` with full env vars, secrets, `--ephemeral-storage=10Gi` |

## Test Coverage

All tests pass (`go test ./...` — 7 packages, all `ok`).

| Package | Coverage | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| `cmd/transcoder` (main) | 64.1% | `main()` and `run()` excluded — bootstrap real infra |
| `cmd/transcoder/internal/ffmpeg` | **100%** | All paths including `ExecCommandRunner` |
| `cmd/transcoder/internal/storage` | 80% | `gcs_client.go` excluded — requires live GCS |
| `cmd/transcoder/internal/video` | **100%** | All paths |

**Tests created:**

- `main_test.go`: 20 tests covering `transcode()` happy path, all 6 error paths (each verified to call `MarkFailed`), and `configFromEnv()` with all required vars missing.
- `internal/ffmpeg/runner_test.go`: 20 tests covering `DefaultRenditions`, `TranscodeHLS` (arg validation, empty renditions, error propagation), `ExtractThumbnail` (offset, frames:v, paths), and `ExecCommandRunner` (success, failure, stderr capture).
- `internal/storage/gcs_test.go`: 13 tests covering `Downloader.Download` (success, bucket/object passthrough, reader error, parent dir creation, copy error) and `Uploader.UploadFile`/`UploadDir` (success, passthrough, missing file, close error, write error, multi-file walk).
- `internal/video/repository_test.go`: 14 tests covering `UpdateVideo` (all arg positions, exec error, zero rows, rows-affected error) and `MarkFailed` (success, arg positions, exec error).

## CI/CD

Existing `.github/workflows/unit-tests.yml` already covers the transcoder — the `test-api` job runs `go test ./...` from `api/`, which includes `cmd/transcoder/...`. No new workflow needed.

items.title: Removing framework stubs
items.body:
Greptile Summary

This PR removes the unimplemented framework stub subsystem (QUA/QubiC) and replaces it with a leaner executor-factory naming convention, while also adding a new periodic `HeartbeatClient` that keeps the QPU status "online" in the Coda cloud. **Key changes:** - Deletes `frameworks/` package (`base.py`, `registry.py`, `qua/`, `qubic/`) — all were stubs that raised `NotImplementedError` - Introduces `_discover_executor_factories()` in `executor.py` — scans installed packages for `.executor_factory:create_executor` and uses it if exactly one match is found - Adds `HeartbeatClient` (`heartbeat.py`) — POSTs authenticated JWT heartbeats on a configurable interval (default 30 s); wired into the FastAPI lifespan in `app.py` - Improves `RedisConsumer` — adds `_decode_fields()` for byte-safe message parsing, periodic `recover_pending()` every 60 s when the stream is idle, and removes the now-unnecessary idle-time filter from recovery (query is already scoped to this consumer) - Removes OPX-specific settings (`opx_host`, `opx_port`) and unused path fields (`register_path`, `self_service_path`); sets a sensible default for `webapp_url`; auto-detects `./site/device.yaml` as the default device config path - Drops `pyyaml` as a core dependency (YAML parsing is now the responsibility of backend packages)

Confidence Score: 4/5

- PR is safe to merge; the one flagged issue is a usability/debuggability concern, not a functional regression. - The refactoring cleanly removes dead stub code, the new HeartbeatClient is well-tested, and the consumer improvements are correct. The only issue found is that import failures during executor auto-discovery are silently swallowed with no warning, which would make debugging broken backend packages difficult but does not affect correctness or runtime stability. - src/self_service/server/executor.py — the bare `except Exception: continue` in `_discover_executor_factories()` should emit a warning log.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | src/self_service/server/executor.py | Replaces framework-based executor loading with a simpler naming-convention auto-discovery scan. One issue: import failures during discovery are silently swallowed with no warning logged. | | src/self_service/server/heartbeat.py | New HeartbeatClient that POSTs authenticated JWT heartbeats on a configurable interval. Clean implementation with proper exception handling in the run loop and graceful close/stop lifecycle. | | src/self_service/server/consumer.py | Adds defensive byte-decoding via _decode_fields(), periodic pending recovery every 60 s when idle, and removes the idle-time filter from recover_pending() since xpending_range is scoped to this consumer. Redis connection errors from the periodic recover_pending() are covered by the surrounding try/except. | | src/self_service/server/app.py | Wires in HeartbeatClient to the lifespan; adds decode_responses=True to the Redis client; extracts optional topology connectivity from the executor for heartbeat payloads. Shutdown ordering is correct. | | src/self_service/server/config.py | Removes register_path, self_service_path, opx_host, opx_port; adds heartbeat_interval_sec; changes webapp_url default to the production URL; adds apply_default_device_config validator to auto-detect ./site/device.yaml. | | src/self_service/frameworks/base.py | Deleted — DeviceConfig Pydantic model and Framework protocol removed as part of replacing the framework subsystem with the simpler executor factory naming convention. | | src/self_service/frameworks/registry.py | Deleted — FrameworkRegistry and entry-point-based discovery replaced by _discover_executor_factories() in executor.py. |

Sequence Diagram

 ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant App as FastAPI Lifespan participant LE as load_executor() participant Disc as _discover_executor_factories() participant HB as HeartbeatClient participant Cloud as Coda Cloud participant RC as RedisConsumer participant Redis as Redis Streams App->>LE: load_executor(settings) alt CODA_EXECUTOR_FACTORY set LE-->>App: _instantiate_factory(explicit path) else auto-discovery LE->>Disc: scan packages_distributions() Disc-->>LE: [matched factories] alt exactly 1 match LE-->>App: _instantiate_factory(discovered) else 0 or 2+ matches LE-->>App: NoopExecutor (+ warning) end end App->>HB: HeartbeatClient(url, qpu_id, jwt, consumer, interval) App->>RC: consume_loop() App->>HB: run() [background task] loop every interval seconds HB->>HB: sign_token(qpu_id, jwt_private_key) HB->>Cloud: POST /heartbeat {current_job, last_job_at, redis_healthy, connectivity} Cloud-->>HB: 200 OK end loop Redis idle (every 60 s) RC->>Redis: XPENDING (this consumer only) Redis-->>RC: pending message IDs RC->>Redis: XRANGE (fetch full message) RC->>RC: _process_message() end App->>HB: close() [shutdown] App->>RC: drain(timeout) [shutdown] ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `src/self_service/server/executor.py`, line 2131-2134 ([link](https://github.com/conductorquantum/coda-self-service/blob/d3c0c621d900910284d39f15d693a7ef849862cf/src/self_service/server/executor.py#L2131-L2134)) **Silent import failure hides broken executor factories** When `find_spec()` finds an `executor_factory` module but `import_module()` fails (e.g., due to a syntax error, missing dependency, or a broken import in the backend package), the exception is silently swallowed with a bare `continue` and no warning is emitted. As a result, the runtime falls back to `NoopExecutor` without any diagnostic message, making this very hard to debug for backend package authors.

items.title: feat: mount Slack on FastAPI, add file extraction and document tools
items.body:
## Summary

Mounts the Slack bot directly into the FastAPI app (previously standalone Socket Mode only), enabling deployment on Vercel and other FaaS platforms. Also adds file extraction from sandboxes and pre-installs document processing tools.

### New capabilities
- **FastAPI mount** (`slack_routes.py`) — Slack Bolt runs as a sub-app on `/slack/events` with `process_before_response=True` for FaaS compatibility
- **File extraction** — Agent-generated files are diffed against the initial sandbox state, extracted, and uploaded back to the Slack thread (up to 10 files, 25 MB each, 50 MB total)
- **File upload limit** bumped from 10 MB to 50 MB for incoming Slack files
- **Document tools** — Sandbox template ships with `poppler-utils`, `qpdf`, `pypdf`, `pdfplumber`, `reportlab`, and `markitdown`

### Config changes
- `timeout` and `system_prompt_append` moved from env vars (`SANDSTORM_SLACK_MODEL`, `SANDSTORM_SLACK_TIMEOUT`) to `sandstorm.json` — single source of truth for all entry points
- `system_prompt_append` supports both string and dict prompt formats
- `LINEAR_API_KEY` forwarded to sandbox when present (for MCP integrations)

### Fixes
- Prevent double run on sandbox reuse failure (generate fresh `run_id` on retry)
- Remove unauthenticated early returns in Slack event handling
- Slack deps (`slack-bolt`, `slack-sdk`, `aiohttp`) moved to core — required for Vercel where optional extras aren't installed

### Deployment
- Vercel `maxDuration` bumped to 300s
- Routes consolidated for the new `/slack/events` endpoint

## Test plan
- [x] `test_slack_routes.py` — event dispatch, file handling, error responses

<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Breaking change

Previously all [actions registered by the supervisor integration](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/hassio/#actions) (`hassio.app_start`, `hassio.backup_partial`, `hassio.host_reboot`, etc.) simply logged an error on failure. The script/automation proceeded regardless of whether that action succeeded. Now they properly raise on failure which means the automation/script will stop unless `continue_on_error` is set to `True`.

## Proposed change

Final task in the long-running `aiohasupervisor` migration - the service calls. These were implemented in a dynamic way but they are static and known at design time so re-implemented them to use aiohasupervisor for all communication with Supervisor.

As the service calls had not been updated in a while, made two small changes. Let me know if one or both of these belong in a different PR and I can break them out:

1. The partial and full backup APIs of Supervisor have always returned the ID of the new backup. As service calls now support responses I updated these services to return the backup ID if users want to use that in their automation.
2. Supervisor's partial backup and partial restore APIs have always been set to reject calls as invalid if the list of `addons` or `folders` contained duplicates. This was not captured in the schema for these service calls. I updated them to raise invalid in this case. I'm not sure if this qualifies as a breaking change though? Since the call would've failed I figured no but it would've at least saved before and now it won't.

items.body:
Thanks for the feedback on #19. I've created this new PR, which merges into #19, to address your comment. I will work on the changes and keep this PR's description up to date as I make progress.

Original PR: #19
Triggering comment (https://github.com/mrdja026/some-kind-of-irc/pull/19#issuecomment-4104104774):
> @copilot

## Changes Made

### More Redis session event logging

Extended `append_ai_session_event` coverage to all AI endpoints that were previously missing session event emission:

- **`ai-service/main.py`**: Added logging to `/ai/calendar/questions` (`calendar_question`), `/ai/calendar/create` (`calendar_create`), and `/ai/local/query` for all outcome paths — greeting (`local_qa_greeting`), rejected (`local_qa_rejected`), and answered (`local_qa_answer`).
- **`ai-service-adk/main.py`**: Added logging to `/ai/calendar/questions` (`calendar_question`) and `/ai/calendar/create` (`calendar_create`).

Previously only the Gmail endpoints emitted events to `ai:session_events`; now all AI endpoints across both services are covered.

### Documentation updates (`Readme.md`)

- Documented the `ai:session_events` Redis stream (maxlen 500, emitted by both `ai-service` and `ai-service-adk`)

items.title: Fix GC% calculation to exclude ambiguous bases + add preprocessing pipeline
items.body:
## Problem

GC% calculation incorrectly included N's (ambiguous bases) in denominator, violating NCBI/Ensembl/UCSC genomic standards. Sequence `ATCGNNNN` returned 25% instead of correct 50%.

## Changes

### Core Fix: GC% Calculation
- **Formula corrected**: `(G+C) / len(seq)` → `(G+C) / (A+T+G+C)`
- **Performance**: Refactored to O(n) single-pass via shared `_count_bases()` helper
- **Applies to**: `calc_gc_content()`, `calc_at_content()` in `detectors_utils.py`

```python
# Before: Incorrect - includes N's
def calc_gc_content(seq):
    gc = sum(1 for c in seq if c in {'G', 'C'})
    return (gc / len(seq)) * 100  # Wrong denominator

# After: Correct - excludes N's
def calc_gc_content(seq):
    a, t, g, c = _count_bases(seq)  # Single pass
    valid = a + t + g + c
    return ((g + c) / valid) * 100 if valid else 0.0
```

### Preprocessing Pipeline
- **Module**: `Utilities/sequence_preprocessor.py`
- **7-step workflow**: Normalization → FASTA parsing → character analysis → GC calculation → validation
- **Returns**: `PreprocessingResult` dataclass with sequence stats, GC%, validation status, error positions
- **IUPAC-aware**: Distinguishes valid (ATGCN), ambiguous (RYKMSWBDHV), and invalid characters

### Integration
- **NonBScanner**: Added opt-in `use_preprocessing` parameter (default: `False` for backward compat)
- **Safety module**: New `generate_sequence_quality_report()` for batch validation

### Testing & Docs
- **Tests**: +33 new tests covering GC calculation, FASTA parsing, validation (105 total, all passing)
- **Docs**: New `docs/SEQUENCE_PREPROCESSING.md` with workflow diagram, scientific basis, API reference

## Impact

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| GC% accuracy | ❌ Incorrect for sequences with N's | ✅ NCBI/Ensembl standard |
| Time complexity | O(4n) | O(n) |
| Breaking changes | N/A | None (opt-in) |

**Example**: Sequence with 50% N content now reports 25 percentage points higher GC% (correctly excludes N's from denominator).

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## 🎯 Objective

Implement a **scientifically rigorous, gold-standard sequence preprocessing pipeline** for genomic DNA analysis that ensures data quality and accuracy before motif detection.

## 📋 Current Issues

### 1. **Incorrect GC% Calculation**
**Current Implementation:**
```python
# Utilities/detectors_utils.py (Line 40-54)
def calc_gc_content(seq: str) -> float:
    if not seq: return 0.0
    gc_count = sum(1 for c in seq if c in {'G', 'C', 'g', 'c'})
    return (gc_count / len(seq)) * 100  # ← WRONG: Uses total length (includes N, gaps)
```

**Problem:** 
- Denominator includes **N's** (ambiguous bases) and potentially **invalid characters**
- For sequence `ATCGNNNATCG` (11 chars), reports GC% = 18.18% instead of correct 25%
- Violates genomic analysis standard: **GC% = (G+C) / (A+T+G+C) × 100**

### 2. **Missing Comprehensive Preprocessing**
Current workflow:
```python
# Utilities/nonbscanner.py (Line 155-157)
sequence = sequence.upper().strip()
is_valid, msg = validate_sequence(sequence)  # Only checks ATCGN
```

**Missing Steps:**
- ❌ No explicit FASTA header removal (handled externally)
- ❌ No multi-line sequence concatenation verification
- ❌ No detailed character counting (A, T, G, C, N, invalid)
- ❌ No positional reporting for invalid characters
- ❌ No pre-analysis sequence statistics

### 3. **Incomplete Validation Reporting**
```python
# Utilities/safety.py (Line 125-156)
def validate_sequence_input(sequences: List[str], min_length: int = 10) -> tuple:
    # Only reports: empty, too short, invalid characters
    # Does NOT report: character counts, N positions, GC%, etc.
```

## ✅ Required Implementation

### **New Module: `Utilities/sequence_preprocessor.py`**

Create a comprehensive preprocessing module with these functions:

#### 1. **`preprocess_sequence(raw_input: str) -> PreprocessingResult`**

**Workflow:**
```python
class PreprocessingResult:
    """Data class for preprocessing results."""
    header: str                          # FASTA header (without '>')
    sequence: str                        # Clean, single-line, uppercase sequence
    length: int                          # Total sequence length
    valid_bases: int                     # Count of A+T+G+C only
    character_counts: Dict[str, int]     # {'A': 1234, 'T': 1456, 'G': 987, 'C': 876, 'N': 45}
    invalid_characters: Dict[str, List[int]]  # {'X': [123, 456], '1': [789]}
    gc_percentage: float                 # (G+C) / (A+T+G+C) × 100
    at_percentage: float                 # (A+T) / (A+T+G+C) × 100
    gc_balance: str                      # "Balanced" | "GC-rich" | "AT-rich"
    validation_status: str               # "valid" | "warning" | "error"
    warnings: List[str]                  # Warning messages
    errors: List[str]                    # Error messages
```

**Processing Steps:**
1. **Uppercase normalization**: `raw_input.upper()`
2. **FASTA header extraction**: Parse lines starting with `>`, extract first header
3. **Multi-line concatenation**: Join all non-header lines into single string
4. **Character analysis**:
   - Count: A, T, G, C separately
   - Count: N (ambiguous base)
   - Detect: Invalid characters (non-IUPAC codes)
   - Report: Positions of first 10 invalid characters per type
5. **Length calculation**: 
   - `total_length = len(sequence)`
   - `valid_bases = count(A) + count(T) + count(G) + count(C)`
6. **GC percentage (CORRECT)**:
   ```python
   if valid_bases == 0:
       raise ValueError("No valid ATGC bases found")
   gc_percentage = ((g_count + c_count) / valid_bases) * 100
   at_percentage = ((a_count + t_count) / valid_bases) * 100
   ```
7. **GC Balance classification**:
   - GC% > 60%: "GC-rich"
   - GC% < 40%: "AT-rich"
   - Otherwise: "Balanced"
8. **Validation**:
   - **Warnings** if N's present (>0%)
   - **Errors** if invalid characters detected
   - **Status**: "valid" (no issues) | "warning" (has N's) | "error" (invalid chars)

#### 2. **`fix_gc_calculation()` - Update Existing Function**

**File**: `Utilities/detectors_utils.py`

**Current (Line 40-54):**
```python
def calc_gc_content(seq: str) -> float:
    if not seq: return 0.0
    gc_count = sum(1 for c in seq if c in {'G', 'C', 'g', 'c'})
    return (gc_count / len(seq)) * 100  # ← FIX THIS
```

**Replace with:**
```python
def calc_gc_content(seq: str) -> float:
    """
    Calculate GC content percentage using genomic standard formula.
    
    Formula: GC% = (G + C) / (A + T + G + C) × 100
    
    Args:
        seq: DNA sequence string (case-insensitive)
        
    Returns:
        GC content as percentage (0-100), or 0.0 if no valid ATGC bases
        
    Scientific Basis:
        - Excludes ambiguous bases (N, R, Y, etc.) from denominator
        - Only counts definitive nucleotides (A, T, G, C)
        - Standard used in genomics (NCBI, Ensembl, UCSC Genome Browser)
        
    Example:
        >>> calc_gc_content("ATCG")
        50.0
        >>> calc_gc_content("ATCGNNNN")  # N's excluded
        50.0  #...

</details>

items.title: Ruff152
Migrate to pyparsing 3.x and drop Python 2.7 support
items.body:
## Summary

This PR migrates pyhocon from pyparsing 2.x to pyparsing 3.x, updating all deprecated API calls to use PEP8-compliant naming conventions. As pyparsing 3.x requires Python 3.6.8+, this also removes Python 2.7 and EOL Python 3.x versions (3.4-3.6) support.

## Motivation

- **Pyparsing 3.3.0+** now emits `DeprecationWarning` for old camelCase method names (e.g., `parseString`, `setParseAction`)
- These deprecated names will be **removed in future pyparsing versions**, which would break pyhocon
- Pyparsing 3.0 was released in **August 2021** - this migration is overdue
- Python 2.7 reached EOL in **January 2020** and Python 3.4-3.6 are also EOL
- This future-proofs pyhocon and aligns with modern Python conventions

## Changes Made

### Dependency Updates
- ✅ Updated pyparsing from `>=2,<4` to `>=3.0.0`
- ✅ Dropped Python 2.7, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6 support
- ✅ Added Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13 support
- ✅ Minimum Python version: **3.7+**

### API Migrations (pyparsing 3.x)

**Method Names (camelCase → snake_case):**
- `parseString()` → `parse_string()`
- `setParseAction()` → `set_parse_action()`
- `setDefaultWhitespaceChars()` → `set_default_whitespace_chars()`
- `replaceWith()` → `replace_with()`

**Parameter Names:**
- `caseless=` → `case_insensitive=`
- `escChar=` → `esc_char=`
- `unquoteResults=` → `unquote_results=`
- `parseAll=` → `parse_all=`

### Code Cleanup
- ✅ Removed `basestring`/`unicode` compatibility shims
- ✅ Removed `urllib2` fallback (now uses `urllib.request` directly)
- ✅ Removed Python < 3.5 `glob` fallback
- ✅ Removed Python < 3.4 `imp` module usage (now uses `importlib.util`)
- ✅ Simplified string type handling (`str` instead of `unicode`)
- ✅ Net reduction: **120 lines of compatibility code removed**

### Files Modified
| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `setup.py` | Updated dependencies and Python version classifiers |
| `tox.ini` | Updated test environments (py37-py313) |
| `pyhocon/config_parser.py` | 17+ pyparsing API updates + Python 2.x removal |
| `pyhocon/period_parser.py` | 3 pyparsing API updates |
| `pyhocon/config_tree.py` | Python 2.x compatibility removal |
| `pyhocon/converter.py` | Python 2.x compatibility removal |

## Testing

- ✅ **All 309 tests pass** with pyparsing 3.3.1
- ✅ **No deprecation warnings** emitted
- ✅ **CLI tool verified working** (`pyhocon` command)
- ✅ Tested with Python 3.13.3
- ✅ No behavioral changes to the pyhocon API

### Test Results
```
============================= test session starts ==============================
platform win32 -- Python 3.13.3, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0
collected 309 items

tests/test_config_parser.py ............................ [truncated]
tests/test_config_tree.py ..............................
tests/test_converter.py ............................
tests/test_periods.py ..................................
tests/test_tool.py ........

======================== 309 passed, 1 xfailed in 5.23s ========================
```

## Breaking Changes ⚠️

This PR introduces **breaking changes** for end users:

### What Breaks
- ❌ Python 2.7 is **no longer supported**
- ❌ Python 3.4, 3.5, 3.6 are **no longer supported**
- ❌ Users on older Python versions must upgrade to **Python 3.7+**
- ❌ Users must have pyparsing **3.0.0+** installed

### What Doesn't Break
- ✅ The **pyhocon API remains unchanged** (e.g., `ConfigFactory.parse_file()` still works)
- ✅ All existing HOCON files continue to parse correctly
- ✅ No changes to configuration syntax or behavior
- ✅ 100% backward compatible for users on Python 3.7+

## Migration Guide for Users

### For Users on Python 3.7+
No code changes required. Simply update dependencies:
```bash
pip install --upgrade pyhocon
```

### For Users on Python 2.7 or 3.4-3.6
You must upgrade Python first:
```bash
# Upgrade to Python 3.7 or later
# Then install pyhocon
pip install --upgrade pyhocon
```

Or pin to the last compatible version:
```bash
pip install 'pyhocon<0.3.62'  # Last version supporting Python 2.7/3.6
```

## Release Notes Suggestion

**Version: 0.4.0** (suggest major/minor bump due to breaking changes)

### Breaking Changes
- Dropped Python 2.7, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6 support
- Minimum Python version is now 3.7
- Minimum pyparsing version is now 3.0.0

### Changes
- Migrated to pyparsing 3.x with PEP8-compliant API
- Removed Python 2.x compatibility code
- Added support for Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13
- Reduced codebase by 120 lines

### Improvements
- Future-proof against pyparsing deprecation warnings
- Cleaner, more maintainable codebase
- Aligned with modern Python conventions

## Checklist

- [x] All tests pass
- [x] No deprecation warnings
- [x] Breaking changes documented
- [x] Migration guide provided
- [x] Code follows PEP8 conventions
- [x] Documentation updated
- [x] Commit message is descriptive

## Additional Context

- **Pyparsing 3.0 Release**: August 2021
- **Python 2.7 EOL**: January 2020
- **Python 3.6 EOL**: December 2021
- **Current pyparsing version**: 3.3.1 (as of testing)

## References

- [Pyparsing 3.0.0 What's New](https://pyparsing-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/whats_new_in_3_0_0.html)
- [Pyparsing PEP-8 Planning](https://github.com/pyparsing/pyparsing/wiki/PEP-8-planning)
- [Python 2.7 EOL Announcement](https://www.python.org/doc/sunset-python-2/)

items.title: Fix ImportError by replacing distlib.compat.raw_input with built-in input()
items.body:
## Problem

When installing `django-tailwind` without optional dependencies, users encounter an `ImportError`:

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'distlib'

This occurs because:
- `distlib` is not listed as a required dependency in `pyproject.toml` (only `django` and
`pytailwindcss` are)
- `distlib` is only available as a transitive dependency through the optional `cookiecutter` package
(`cookiecutter` → `virtualenv` → `distlib`)
- The management command imports `from distlib.compat import raw_input` at the module level

I encountered this issue when building a Docker image with `pip install django-tailwind`, which
doesn't include optional dependencies.

## Solution

Since `django-tailwind` requires Python 3.11+ (as specified in `pyproject.toml`), we can safely use
Python 3's built-in `input()` function instead of the Python 2/3 compatibility wrapper.

**Changes made:**
- Removed `from distlib.compat import raw_input` import
- Replaced all 3 occurrences of `raw_input()` with `input()`

In Python 3, `input()` behaves identically to Python 2's `raw_input()` (returns a string without
evaluation).

## Testing

- ✅ All existing tests pass (`uv run pytest`)
- ✅ Code formatted with ruff
- ✅ Verified the import no longer depends on an unlisted dependency

This fix ensures users can install and use `django-tailwind` with only the required dependencies
listed in `pyproject.toml`.

Adds OAuth2-based authentication for Claude Pro/Max subscriptions alongside the existing API key path, enabling the proxy to use Claude subscription tokens under the unified anthropic/ model prefix.

New files:
- anthropic_oauth_base.py: Token management base class with PKCE OAuth flow, token refresh via JSON POST, claudeAiOauth credential file parsing (ms→s expiry normalisation), env var credential support, queue-based refresh coordination, and interactive credential setup
- anthropic_provider.py: Dual-path provider — OAuth credentials route via direct httpx calls to Anthropic Messages API with bearer token + beta headers, tool mcp_ prefix handling, streaming SSE conversion, and thinking block support; API key credentials delegate unchanged to litellm

Modified files:
- credential_manager.py: add anthropic to DEFAULT_OAUTH_DIRS (~/.claude) and ENV_OAUTH_PROVIDERS (ANTHROPIC_OAUTH prefix)

- credential_tool.py: add "Claude / Claude Code (Pro & Max)" to OAUTH_FRIENDLY_NAMES; switch hardcoded oauth_providers list to provider_factory.get_available_providers() so all registered OAuth providers (including future ones) appear in view/delete/edit menus

items.title: Fix ansible 2.20.xx deprecation warnings
items.body:
##### SUMMARY
ansible 2.20 deprecates importing from `ansible.module_utils._text`. This displays warnings to users of the modules.

For instance, this tasks:
```
- name: 'Create a postgres user for Debian-exim'
  community.postgresql.postgresql_user:
    name: 'Debian-exim'
  become: true
  become_user: 'postgres'
```

yields these warnings:
> TASK [postgresql : Create a postgres user for Debian-exim] *********************                                                                                 
> [WARNING]: Deprecation warnings can be disabled by setting `deprecation_warnings=False` in ansible.cfg.                                                          
> [DEPRECATION WARNING]: Importing 'to_bytes' from 'ansible.module_utils._text' is deprecated. This feature will be removed from ansible-core version 2.24. Use ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters instead.                                                                                                                
> [DEPRECATION WARNING]: Importing 'to_native' from 'ansible.module_utils._text' is deprecated. This feature will be removed from ansible-core version 2.24. Use ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters instead.                                                                                                               
> [DEPRECATION WARNING]: Importing 'to_text' from 'ansible.module_utils._text' is deprecated. This feature will be removed from ansible-core version 2.24. Use ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters instead.                                                                                                                 
> ok: [instance]

This commit fixes all related warning by using `ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters` as requested by ansible.

##### ISSUE TYPE
- Bugfix Pull Request

##### COMPONENT NAME
Happens in various places

items.title: NSCK V10: full-stack cognitive extensions, Rust concurrent memory, and final audit
items.body:
Implements all Tier 1–3 roadmap items from the V9 report: Rust concurrent memory exposed, dense embedding↔VSA bridge, differentiable FHRR VSA, probabilistic NLU, attention-weighted GWT, neural rule scoring, safety verification, and a REST API. Follows with a comprehensive quality audit fixing latent bugs, dead code, and bringing all docs current.

## V10 New Modules

| Module | What it does |
|---|---|
| `vsa/vsa_embedding_bridge.py` | Random-projection bridge: 768-dim dense → 10,240-bit HV and back; sentence-transformers optional |
| `vsa/fhrr.py` | Complex-phasor VSA — bind=multiply, unbind=conjugate-mul; gradient-compatible via `to_gradient_input()` |
| `vsa/rust_concurrent_shim.py` | Exposes `SemanticMemoryConcurrent`, `EpisodicMemoryConcurrent`, `CognitiveWorkerPool` from rebuilt Rust `.so`; Python fallbacks |
| `language/ngram_nlu.py` | Naive Bayes n-gram NLU (33K sent/s); intent + entity extraction |
| `reasoning/attention_gwt_bridge.py` | Multi-head attention weights coalition salience before GWT competition |
| `learning/rule_neural_scorer.py` | Single-hidden-layer perceptron re-ranks rules; online SGD; wired into `decide()` via `enable_neural_rule_scoring()` |
| `cognitive/safety_verifier.py` | Formula-based safety properties in restricted `eval` namespace; `SafetyGateVerifier.gate_decision()` |
| `api/nsck_api.py` | FastAPI REST wrapper (POST `/decide`, `/learn`, `/sleep`; GET `/status`); stdlib fallback |

## Rust

Both `hypervec_rs` and `snn_rs` rebuilt. `hypervec_rs` now exports `SemanticMemoryConcurrent`, `EpisodicMemoryConcurrent`, `Episode`, `CognitiveWorkerPool`, `HyperVectorRegistry`, `PersistentStorage`, `parallel_bundle`, `batch_parallel_similarity_search` — previously compiled but not wired to Python.

```python
from python.core.vsa.rust_concurrent_shim import SemanticMemoryConcurrent, USE_RUST_CONCURRENT
# USE_RUST_CONCURRENT=True when .so is present; falls back to pure-Python transparently
sm = SemanticMemoryConcurrent()
sm.add_concept("cat", hv)
```

## Wiring into `CognitiveEngine.decide()`

Neural rule scorer integrates as an opt-in re-ranker; score is taken from the same post-ranking position to avoid rule/confidence mismatch:

```python
engine.enable_neural_rule_scoring()
# decide() now: get applicable rules → neural re-rank → take top rule AND its score
```

## Quality Audit

- **Bug**: `Rule` is a non-frozen `@dataclass` (`__hash__ = None`); using rule objects as dict keys crashed the neural scorer. Fixed with identity comparison (`r is rule`).
- **Bug**: `api/nsck_api.py` `handle_decide` propagated exceptions uncaught. Wrapped in try/except; errors returned as structured JSON.
- **Dead code**: no-op `for prop in DEFAULT_PROPERTIES: pass` loop in `SafetyGateVerifier.__init__` removed.
- **Test warnings**: 3 `PytestReturnNotNoneWarning`s eliminated (`return True`/`return bool` → `assert`).

## Documentation

- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — §15 V10 module table and updated decision loop
- `docs/MODULE_REFERENCE.md` — full V10 section (all 8 modules, classes, methods, signatures)
- `docs/WORKFLOWS.md` — §9 neural scoring, safety gate, FHRR+embedding, REST API sequence diagrams
- `docs/NSCK_V10_EXTENSIONS.md` — new end-to-end V10 spec
- `README.md` — V10 capabilities and quick-start

**1,248 tests pass with Rust active · 0 CodeQL alerts · warnings 4 → 1**

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Goal

Transform NSCK from a "collection of cognitive modules" into a **true modality-agnostic cognitive substrate** — inherently capable of learning, reasoning, remembrance, recall, generalization, cross-domain transfer, and lifelong learning for **any input type** (numbers, text, audio, images, sensor streams, dicts, etc.).

The constraint: **no neural nets in the understanding/reasoning/memory/decision core**. Neural components (SNN) are only allowed as optional perception adapters at the edge. The core remains purely VSA + symbolic.

## Branch

Base branch: `NSCK_V3`

## The 7 Implementation Steps

### STEP 1: Universal PerceptPacket Contract

**Problem:** Currently `decide()` in `cognitive_engine.py` (line ~438) accepts raw `Dict[str, Any]` and does perception inline — calling `GroundingVerifier.get_active_predicates(state)` and `EpisodicMemory.create_situation_hv(state, ...)` inside the decision loop. This means perception and cognition are tangled. Each new modality must know how `decide()` works internally.

**What to implement:**

1. Create `nsck/python/core/types/percept_packet.py` with a frozen dataclass `PerceptPacket`:
   - `modality: str` ("text", "numeric", "dict", "snn", "multimodal", "stream")
   - `timestamp: float`
   - `situation_hv: HyperVector` (bundled summary)
   - `entity_hvs: Dict[str, HyperVector]` (named concept → HV)
   - `relation_hvs: List[Tuple[str, str, str, HyperVector]]` (subj, pred, obj, triple_hv)
   - `active_predicates: FrozenSet[str]` (grounded symbolic predicates)
   - `confidence: float` (0.0–1.0)
   - `raw_state: Optional[Dict[str, Any]]` (original state dict for backward compat)
   - `adapter_name: str`
   - `adapter_trace: Dict[str, Any]`

2. Create `nsck/python/core/types/modality_adapter.py` with abstract base class `ModalityAdapter` having one method: `encode(raw_input: Any, task_tag: str) -> PerceptPacket`

3. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/dict_state_adapter.py` — wraps the existing `GroundingVerifier` + `EpisodicMemory.create_situation_hv()` logic currently inline in `decide()` lines 458-473.

4. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/text_adapter.py` — wraps `UniversalInput.ground_text()`.

5. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/numeric_adapter.py` — wraps `UniversalInput.ground_scalar()` and `ground_sequence()`.

6. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/snn_adapter.py` — wraps the logic from `perceive_and_decide()` lines 366-427.

7. Modify `CognitiveEngine.decide()` to accept `Union[Dict, PerceptPacket]` — if dict, auto-wrap via the registered adapter (backward compatible). If PerceptPacket, use directly. The core body of `decide()` from line ~473 onward (curiosity check, coalition building, GWT, safety, explanation) stays unchanged but reads from `percept.active_predicates` and `percept.situation_hv` instead of computing them inline.

8. Modify `register_task()` to also register an adapter (auto-creates `DictStateAdapter` from verifier if no adapter provided).

9. Add `self.adapters: Dict[str, ModalityAdapter] = {}` to `CognitiveEngine.__init__()`.

**Backward compatibility:** ALL existing tests must pass unchanged because `decide()` auto-wraps dicts.

### STEP 2: Wire Generalization Into the Automatic Learning Loop

**Problem:** `SemanticMemory.build_prototypes()`, `infer_transitive()`, `extract_schema()`, and `AnalogyEngine.auto_discover_abstractions()` all exist as callable methods but are NEVER invoked automatically by `sleep()` or `learn()`. Generalization is "available" but not "inherent."

**What to implement:**

Add to `CognitiveEngine.sleep()` (after existing step 3 "_consolidate_semantic" and before step 4 "planner refresh"):

- **Step 4a: Prototype building** — call `self.semantic_memory.build_prototypes(min_members=2)` and store results back.
- **Step 4b: Transitive inference** — call `self.semantic_memory.infer_transitive("is_a", max_hops=3)` and `infer_transitive("causes", max_hops=2)`.
- **Step 4c: Cross-task auto-discovery** — for every pair of tasks being consolidated, call `self.analogy.auto_discover_abstractions()` with concept HVs from both tasks.

Add helper `_collect_concept_hvs_for_task(task_tag)` that gathers HVs from rules + causal graph nodes.

Track new stats: `prototypes_built`, `transitive_inferences`, `auto_abstractions`.

### STEP 3: Automatic Cross-Domain Transfer on New Task Registration

**Problem:** `register_task()` (line ~298) only sets up verifier, causal graph, and causal reasoner. No transfer attempt happens. The `transfer()` method exists separately and must be called manually.

**What to implement:**

At the end of `register_task()`, if `self.config.enable_auto_transfer` is True and there are known tasks with learned rules:
- For each known task, call `self.analogy.auto_discover_abstractions()` between it and the new task.
- If mappings found, call `self.analogy.transfer_rule()` for each learned rule and inject transferred rules into `rule_learner`.
- Add `add_transferred_rule(task_tag, condition, consequence, sour...

</details>

items.title: plot_channel_contribution_grid to MMM Multidimensional + Documentation Notebook

milestone.description: After some time on developing cycles and fine tunning the APIs. We want to bring a release that stabilized the API for all modules

items.body:
Add `plot_channel_contribution_grid` to MMM Multidimensional for counterfactual spend analysis

## PR Description

### Summary

This PR adds the `plot_channel_contribution_grid` method and supporting infrastructure to the `MMM` class in `pymc_marketing.mmm.multidimensional`, enabling users to visualize how channel contributions change across different spend levels through counterfactual analysis.

### What's New

#### New Methods in `MMM` (multidimensional.py)

1. **`get_channel_contribution_forward_pass_grid`** - Computes channel contributions for a grid of spend multipliers (deltas). This method:
   - Takes `start`, `stop`, and `num` parameters to define a multiplier grid (e.g., 0x to 2x current spend)
   - Scales the original channel data by each delta value
   - Uses `sample_posterior_predictive` to compute contributions for each scenario
   - Returns an `xr.DataArray` with dims `(delta, chain, draw, date, *dims, channel)`

2. **`plot_channel_contribution_grid`** - High-level plotting method that:
   - Computes the contribution grid internally
   - Supports `absolute_xrange` to show absolute spend values instead of multipliers
   - Supports `dims` filtering (e.g., `{"country": "US"}`)
   - Supports `aggregation` operations (e.g., `{"sum": ("country",)}`)
   - Delegates visualization to the plot suite

3. **`_build_prediction_dataframe`** - Helper method to convert channel xarray data to DataFrame format for `sample_posterior_predictive`

#### New Method in `MMMPlotSuite` (plot.py)

- **`channel_contribution_grid`** - Visualization method that:
  - Plots mean contribution and HDI bands for each channel across delta values
  - Handles dimension filtering and aggregation
  - Creates subplots for each dimension combination
  - Adds reference lines (δ=1 for relative, current spend for absolute)

### New Tutorial Notebook

Added `docs/source/notebooks/mmm/media_saturation.ipynb` - A comprehensive tutorial that:
- Explains the difference between **direct/marginal contribution** (`saturation_scatterplot`) and **total contribution over spend share** (`plot_channel_contribution_grid`)
- Clarifies a common source of confusion: these visualizations answer different questions
- Demonstrates basic and advanced usage including dimension filtering and aggregation
- Provides side-by-side comparison of both visualization approaches

### Key Distinction Explained in Tutorial

| Aspect | `saturation_scatterplot` | `plot_channel_contribution_grid` |
|--------|--------------------------|----------------------------------|
| **X-axis** | Spend at each time point | Spend multiplier or total spend |
| **Y-axis** | Direct contribution per time point | Total contribution summed over time |
| **Question answered** | What is the marginal contribution at each spend level? | What happens to total impact if I change budget? |

### Tests

Added comprehensive test coverage in `tests/mmm/test_multidimensional.py`:
- `TestGetChannelContributionForwardPassGrid` - Tests for grid computation, shape validation, error handling, and multi-dimensional support
- `TestPlotChannelContributionGrid` - Tests for basic plotting, absolute x-range, dimension filtering, and aggregation

### Gallery Update

Added the new media saturation tutorial to the documentation gallery.

items.title: [bugfix] Fix FilteredTopK overflow correctness
items.body:
<!-- .github/pull_request_template.md -->

## 📌 Description

 This PR fixes issue #2604: FilteredTopK could produce incorrect results when the refine candidate buffer
  overflows in long-sequence, tie-heavy workloads. 

### Root Cause

FilteredTopK uses a fixed-size refine candidate buffer (SMEM_INPUT_SIZE=16K), which can overflow when the threshold bin is too large. The previous logic could continue with partially truncated state after overflow, causing incorrect top-k outputs.

### Main Changes

#### 1) Kernel correctness fixes (include/flashinfer/topk.cuh)

- Added a refine-overflow flag s_refine_overflow, set atomically on candidate write overflow.
-  On refine-buffer overflow, we now use a full-scan correctness fallback: rebuild the needed histogram/threshold state from the full input, recompute pivot selection state, reset partial intermediate state, and recollect winners from the full
  input to guarantee correct top-k results.

#### 2) Regression test coverage (tests/utils/test_topk.py)

- Added BF16 long-sequence regressions across auto / multi_cta / filtered.
- Added FP32 long-sequence refine-overflow regressions for both top_k and transform APIs (page_table / ragged).
- Added FP32 pivot reconstruction regressions for both top_k and transform APIs.


## 🔍 Related Issues

fix: https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/issues/2604

items.title: Update pillar/grain master cache logic
items.body:
### What does this PR do?

When executing _get_minion_grains and all nodes are loaded via cache, len(missed_minions) == 0.
This causes  ```lret = self._get_live_minion_grains(missed_minions)```  to execute with an empty list which results in the print statement ```No minions matched the target. No command was sent, no jid was assigned.``` but everything else executes fine...
This adds additional validation before executing the fallback statements.

This also ensures that directly queried minion grains/pillars "lret" overwrites any cached data "cret" before return.

items.title: Harden email detection against edge cases and malformed model outputs
items.body:
Email detection could fail on edge cases like `None`, empty strings, or unexpected model outputs. While PR #12 fixed the core issue (binary classification instead of URL's 4-category LABEL_MAP), the edge case handling remained fragile.

## Changes

**`pages/Email Detection.py` - `_interpret_email_label()`**
- Fixed logic flaw: `not model_label and model_label != 0` incorrectly evaluated `False` as an edge case
- Changed to explicit checks: `if model_label is None or (isinstance(model_label, str) and not model_label.strip())`
- Added `.strip()` for whitespace handling
- Added warning logs for debugging unexpected values

**`utils/predictor.py` - `predict_email()`**
- Added validation defaulting to `0` (non-spam) for unexpected outputs
- Changed `label = str(raw_label)` → `label = str(label_int)` to ensure validation applies
- Prevents crashes from malformed model outputs

```python
# Before: Could crash on unexpected model output
label = str(raw_label)  # raw_label might be invalid

# After: Validates and defaults to safe value
label_int = int(raw_label) if int(raw_label) in (0, 1) else 0
label = str(label_int)
```

No changes to happy path behavior - valid inputs (`0`, `1`) work identically.

items.title: New xrange picker, Fixes FPBASE-626, FPBASE-60H
Migrate to Python 3.8+
items.body:
## Summary
This PR migrates Courier from Python 2 to Python 3.8+, making the framework compatible with modern Python versions and laying the foundation for further modernization.

## Changes Made

### Setup Configuration
- ✅ Updated `setup.py` to require Python >= 3.8
- ✅ Added Python version classifiers (3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12)
- ✅ Added `long_description_content_type` for better PyPI rendering
- ✅ Use `find_packages()` for automatic package discovery

### Python 2 → Python 3 Syntax Updates
- ✅ Replaced `basestring` → `str` in `api_method.py:225`
- ✅ Replaced `unicode()` → `str()` in `api_method.py:111`
- ✅ Replaced `.iteritems()` → `.items()` in `__init__.py:79`
- ✅ Updated `urllib.unquote` → `urllib.parse.unquote` in `validation.py`
- ✅ Updated `urllib.unquote_plus` → `urllib.parse.unquote_plus` in `api_method.py`
- ✅ Fixed method introspection: `im_class` → `__self__.__class__` in `__init__.py:87`

### Import Fixes
- ✅ Fixed relative import: `from courier_base` → `from courier.courier_base`

### Version
- ✅ Bumped version from 0.1.0 → 0.2.0

## Breaking Changes
- ⚠️ **Drops Python 2.x support entirely**
- ⚠️ Minimum Python version is now 3.8

## Testing
This PR does not include tests yet. Tests will be added in **PR #4**.

Manual testing should verify:
- [x] No syntax errors when importing courier
- [x] Basic routing functionality works
- [x] WSGI interface remains compatible

## How to Review
1. Check all Python 2 specific syntax has been removed
2. Verify imports are correct and absolute
3. Confirm setup.py metadata is accurate
4. Ensure no regressions in existing functionality

## Related PRs
- **Depends on:** None (base PR)
- **Required by:** PR #2 (Dependency Updates)

## Migration Notes
This PR maintains API compatibility - existing applications using Courier should work without code changes (aside from requiring Python 3.8+).

items.title: Harden PSD/stage analysis, avoid pandas interpolate deprecation, and make pipeline config/result handling more robust
items.body:
### Motivation
- Prevent runtime warnings and future breakage from `DataFrame.interpolate` on object dtypes. 
- Make PSD and stage analysis tolerant to partial/short recordings and missing analyzed artifacts to avoid crashes during merging. 
- Preserve per-dataset output layout and allow a sane default config location for Docker runs. 
- Improve path handling to support alternative FASTER2 result layouts and avoid NameError/XRange issues in stage-time plotting.

### Description
- Call `infer_objects(copy=False)` before `interpolate` in `faster2lib/summary_psd.py` to avoid the pandas deprecation warning and handle object dtypes. 
- Normalize mask/epoch handling and support `range`/`slice`/lists for `epoch_range`, trim ranges to available data, align mask lengths, and return empty `DataFrame`s for empty inputs in `faster2lib/summary_psd.py`. 
- Add fallbacks and existence checks in `analysis.py` to use alternative stat/PSD paths, warn and skip missing files, and return empty frames when no inputs are found. 
- Harden `pipeline_step2_analyze.py` by resolving result directories (`resolve_result_dir`), using `Path`, trimming stage arrays for hourly/circadian profiles and swtrans, making `extract_raw_EEG_n_EMG` resilient, and emitting warnings instead of crashing; also generate per-dataset `analyzed` outputs instead of flattening them. 
- Add `DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH` and `resolve_config_path` in `run_pipeline.py` and update CLI to default to `/data/config.json`, and update `README.md` Docker instructions accordingly.

### Testing
- No automated unit tests or CI were executed for these changes. 
- The change to call `infer_objects` was applied to silence the reported `FutureWarning` from `pandas.DataFrame.interpolate`. 
- Runtime-guarding additions were limited to existence checks and dtype conversions and thus require integration testing on real datasets. 
- Manual review/commit was performed but no automated verification was run.

------

items.title: Add LLM-based command interpreter with Gemini function calling
items.body:
Implements Google Gemini integration as alternative to rule-based pattern matching. LLM adapter interprets natural language commands via function calling and converts responses to existing Intent objects.

## Architecture

**Domain Layer** (`app/domain/services/agent_service.py`)
- Function schemas mapping ActionProvider capabilities (type_text, press_key, open_browser, open_url, search_on_page)
- System instruction defining "Xerife" persona (concise, productivity-focused)
- Function-to-CommandType mapping

**Infrastructure Layer** (`app/adapters/infrastructure/gemini_adapter.py`)
- Drop-in replacement for CommandInterpreter interface
- AsyncIO support via `asyncio.to_thread()` prevents voice loop blocking
- Handles clarification requests by invoking `VoiceProvider.speak()` when confidence is low
- Converts Gemini function calls to Intent objects

**Container** (`app/container.py`)
- `use_llm` flag toggles between rule-based and LLM modes
- Configuration via `GEMINI_API_KEY` and `GEMINI_MODEL` environment variables

## Usage

```python
from app.container import create_edge_container

# Enable LLM mode
container = create_edge_container(use_llm=True)
assistant = container.assistant_service
assistant.start()
```

The LLM interprets commands through function calling:
```
User: "xerife escreva meu nome"
→ Gemini calls type_text(text="meu nome")
→ Adapter converts to Intent(TYPE_TEXT, {"text": "meu nome"})
→ Existing IntentProcessor/AssistantService execute unchanged
```

## Implementation Notes

- Preserves hexagonal architecture: domain layer remains dependency-free
- Backward compatible: both modes coexist, selectable at runtime
- 25 new tests (11 AgentService, 14 LLMCommandAdapter), all existing tests pass
- No security vulnerabilities (CodeQL clean, dependency scan clean)

## Configuration

Add to `.env`:
```env
GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-1.5-flash
```

See `LLM_INTEGRATION.md` for detailed guide.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Atue como Especialista em IA. Vamos substituir o CommandInterpreter atual (baseado em regras) por um LLMCommandAdapter usando a API do Google Gemini.
> No app/domain/services/, crie uma lógica de 'Agente' que utiliza Function Calling.
> O Agente deve conhecer as capacidades do ActionProvider (type_text, press_key, open_browser).
> Crie um novo Adapter em app/adapters/infrastructure/gemini_adapter.py. Ele deve:
> Usar google-generativeai.
> Converter a resposta da IA em um objeto Intent que o nosso IntentProcessor já entende.
> Utilize AsyncIO para que a chamada à API da IA não trave o loop de voz.
> Adicione uma instrução de sistema (System Instruction) para o modelo agir como 'Xerife', um assistente focado em produtividade e automação, sendo conciso e eficiente.
> Certifique-se de que, se a IA não tiver certeza do comando, ela peça clarificação via VoiceProvider.speak()."


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

items.title: chore(deps): bump pathable from 0.4.4 to 0.5.0
items.body:
Bumps [pathable](https://github.com/p1c2u/pathable) from 0.4.4 to 0.5.0.
Release notes

Sourced from pathable's releases.

0.5.0

Features

  • Added richer accessor/path APIs, including AccessorPath.read_value, subscriptable accessors, improved AccessorPath.get overloads, NodeAccessor.getitem, pathlib-style path manipulation, and traversability support. #29 #31 #85 #93 #66 #89 #91
  • Improved performance in core hot paths, including faster parse_parts, optimized getitem, faster membership checks, and better per-instance caching behavior. #92 #104 #103 #87 #71
  • Expanded parsing and typing guarantees with stronger parsing contracts and broader bytes/PathLike test coverage. #80 #81

Fixes

  • Improved correctness and diagnostics across accessors and paths, including tighter KeyError messages and more consistent len()/keys() behavior. #84 #75 #73
  • Improved filesystem/stat error handling with better OSError handling in accessor flows. #69
  • Fixed separator correctness in core path operations. #67
  • Fixed AccessorPath child-return behavior for getitem. #94

Backward incompatibilities

  • Dropped Python 3.7, 3.8, and 3.9 support. #24 #25 #101
  • Python baseline is now 3.10+. #101
  • BaseAccessor has been replaced by NodeAccessor. #39
  • AccessorPath is now generic. #39

Deprecations

  • Deprecated legacy accessor/path methods: iter, iteritems, content, get, getkey. #29
  • Deprecated content in favor of newer accessor patterns. #13

Maintenance and tooling

  • Removed pyrsistent dependency. #82
  • Added benchmark suite and CI benchmark workflow. #77 #102
  • Updated development and CI tooling (mypy, pytest, pytest-cov, flynt, pre-commit, black, isort, GitHub Actions components). #95 #96 #97 #98 #99 #100 #59 #58 #56 #54 #53 #51 #49 #48 #47 #42 #41 #36 #35 #34 #19 #18 #17 #16 #23 #22 #21 #20
